# Strait

> Open-source job orchestration for background jobs, scheduled work, private workers, and multi-step workflows.

Strait coordinates work that runs on your infrastructure. It handles job triggers, retries, schedules, workflow steps, approvals, live run updates, and `dead_letter` recovery. Use it when a message queue or cron job no longer gives you enough control over execution history and failure handling.

Strait ships as a single Go binary with PostgreSQL as the source of truth and Redis for live updates. It never runs your code itself: jobs reach your services over an HTTP endpoint Strait calls, or over a long-lived worker that connects to the API by gRPC and keeps your code inside a private network. The binary runs in `api`, `worker`, or `all` mode, and comes in a community (self-hosted) and a cloud edition.

## Start Here

- Introduction: https://docs.strait.dev/introduction
- Choose Your Path: https://docs.strait.dev/choose-your-path
- Quickstart: https://docs.strait.dev/quickstart
